channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 📍 Bookshelf: 2026 looks awfully like 1914 | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Many analysts may say the world is in a new Cold War, but it isn’t, writes Odd Arne Westad in his new book, The Coming Storm: Power, Conflict and Warnings from History. Westad, a renowned ...

item.title: 🏴 In shift, Trump announces deployment of 5,000 US troops to Poland | Defense News
item.description: A week after cancelling a U.S. Army deployment to Poland, Trump announces an additional 5,000 troops will be sent to the country.

item.title: 🏴 As Russian losses mount in Ukraine, Putin seeks more foreign fighters | Atlantic Council
item.description: With no end in sight to the invasion of Ukraine, Putin is looking to recruit more foreign fighters to counter heavy Russian losses while avoiding a politically risky mobilization, write Marc Goedemans and Katherine Spencer. The post As Russian losses mount in Ukraine, Putin…

item.title: 📍 With the Strait of Hormuz closed, the Panama Canal is busier than ever. It should use this windfall wisely. | Atlantic Council
item.description: Panama must use its revenues smartly by reinvesting in upkeep and in solutions that efficiently use the limited water resources. The post With the Strait of Hormuz closed, the Panama Canal is busier than ever. It should use this windfall wisely. appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🏴 Trump Hasn’t Given Up on Greenland | Foreign Policy
item.description: And America’s NATO allies are still on edge about it.

item.title: 🏛️ General Atomics resumes drone-wingman flights after mishap | Defense One
item.description: An investigation by the Air Force and the defense contractor led to a software change.

item.title: 📍 Inside SOCOM’s Search For New Machine Guns, Rifles And Ammo | The War Zone
item.description: Lt. Col. Alan Wood, SOCOM's Program Manager for Lethality, offers some exclusive insights into the future of SOCOM small arms. The post Inside SOCOM’s Search For New Machine Guns, Rifles And Ammo appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 Boots on the moon needed to beat ‘belligerent’ China: Mitchell Institute | Breaking Defense
item.description: “[F]uture American space security is at risk. China’s military-led human space flight progression is positioning the People’s Liberation Army to achieve strategic advantage in lunar access, infrastructure, and resources,” Kyle Puma, Mitchell Institute senior resident fellow for…

item.title: 📍 Every country should understand space as a warfighting domain | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Touring Normandy beaches shortly after D-Day in 1944, General Dwight D Eisenhower reportedly said, ‘If I didn’t have air supremacy, I wouldn’t be here.’ Today, space power has achieved the decisiveness of air power, and ...

item.title: 🏛️ Poland joins Pentagon’s counter-drone marketplace amid unexpected US deployment cancellation | Defense News
item.description: The Army is continuing to expand military cooperation with Poland through the Pentagon’s counter-drone initiative, the service announced on Wednesday.

item.title: 🌍 Israeli Minister’s Taunting of Detained Flotilla Activists Sparks Foreign Backlash | Foreign Policy
item.description: Foreign leaders and Israeli officials are condemning Itamar Ben-Gvir’s treatment of Global Sumud Flotilla activists.

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon considers restoring Army aviation cuts | Breaking Defense
item.description: Pentagon reporter Ashley Roque shares three things to know about the U.S. Army’s latest aviation plans.

item.title: 🕵️ Night Stalker MH-47 Chinooks May Get Aerial Refueling Tanker Role | The War Zone
item.description: Special ops MH-47 Chinooks that could refuel other aircraft midair would give the Night Stalkers their own organic tanker capability. The post Night Stalker MH-47 Chinooks May Get Aerial Refueling Tanker Role appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Strait of Hormuz escort missions would ‘exceed’ Navy’s capacity, CNO says | Breaking Defense
item.description: President Donald Trump had floated the idea of a naval escort months ago, but Adm. Daryl Caudle said today it would be a “very challenging mission.”

item.title: 📍 Georgia’s Dogs Are Disappearing | Foreign Policy
item.description: A botched government program has become a PR disaster.

item.title: 🏴 No, Russia’s Economy Is Not About to Collapse | Foreign Policy
item.description: The war with Ukraine has slowed growth, but Moscow remains stable.

item.title: 📍 The White House Ballroom Is A Deep Fortress In Disguise (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: We now know the building goes six floors underground and will have "the greatest drone empire you've ever seen that's going to protect Washington" on its roof. The post The White House Ballroom Is A Deep Fortress In Disguise (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 For a sustained ceasefire or truce in Sudan, remember: Trust, but verify | Atlantic Council
item.description: Negotiators should insist any ceasefire or truce agreement allows for a dedicated, on-the-ground monitoring mechanism. The post For a sustained ceasefire or truce in Sudan, remember: Trust, but verify appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 To train for contested environments, SOCOM and SOUTHCOM want more ranges, authority | Breaking Defense
item.description: “[W]e have to find places where these kind of more sophisticated projections of force can be rehearsed and can be tested and tried,” Adm. Frank Bradley said.

item.title: 📍 U.S. Military’s Shahed-136 Kamikaze Drone Clone Is Getting Hivemind Swarming Capability | The War Zone
item.description: Shield AI is providing the software to unlock the combat-proven LUCAS's cooperative swarming potential, which could drastically increase its lethality. The post U.S. Military’s Shahed-136 Kamikaze Drone Clone Is Getting Hivemind Swarming Capability appeared first on The War…

item.title: 🏛️ The US Navy is full speed ahead on building a laser fleet | Defense News
item.description: Operation Epic Fury may have strengthened the case for laser weapons, but the U.S. Navy’s dream of putting one on every ship may take longer than expected.

item.title: 🌏 When Internships Enable Child Labor in China | The Diplomat
item.description: Internships are mandatory for the roughly 4 million children who graduate from secondary vocational schools each year. But some internships involve hazardous conditions – and even death.

item.title: 🕵️ Carole House testifies to House Financial Services Committee on modernizing illicit finance frameworks for the twenty-first century | Atlantic Council
item.description: Carole House testified to the House Committee on Financial Services Subcommittee on National Security, Illicit Finance, and International Financial Institutions at a hearing titled, “Modernizing the BSA for Financial Crime in the 21st Century." The post Carole House testifies to…

item.title: 🏛️ Key Army efforts pinned to lawmakers’ taste for a new reconciliation bill | Defense One
item.description: White House puts funding for munitions, industrial development in precedent-breaking request.

item.title: 🏛️ Enemy submarines are harder to detect, but next-gen sonobuoys search them out | Breaking Defense
item.description: [Sponsored] As adversary submarines grow quieter and more elusive, the Navy turns to a new generation of more capable, connected, and open sonobuoys.

item.title: 🏴 Dispatch from Greece: Geopolitical crises are accelerating collaboration between Europe and the Gulf | Atlantic Council
item.description: A look behind the scenes at the recent Europe Gulf Forum, hosted by the Antenna Group in partnership with the Atlantic Council. The post Dispatch from Greece: Geopolitical crises are accelerating collaboration between Europe and the Gulf appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 Montenegro at twenty: What kind of state will it become? | Atlantic Council
item.description: Even as Montenegrins mark today’s anniversary, the true meaning of independence must be something far more ambitious. The post Montenegro at twenty: What kind of state will it become? appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🛡️ How the Domestic Terrorist Label Endangers Rights and Drives Extremist Violence | Just Security
item.description: Prepared congressional testimony for a Senate hearing that was postponed. The post How the Domestic Terrorist Label Endangers Rights and Drives Extremist Violence appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏛️ Air Force dubs MQ-9 the ‘MVP’ of Epic Fury as lawmakers press manned-unmanned future | Defense News
item.description: The remotely piloted MQ-9 Reaper was the workhorse in the U.S. air campaign against Iran, Air Force leaders told Congress on May 20.

item.title: 🌍 The Middle East War Will Reshape Africa’s Democratic Trajectory | Just Security
item.description: The economic fallout across Africa from the Iran war will have major impact on the well-being of African citizens and the prospects for democracy on the continent. The post The Middle East War Will Reshape Africa’s Democratic Trajectory appeared first on Just Security.
